message:
feat(analytics): packet-driven Statistics::from_packets sink [coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.32]

ibuuh.32 sink #2 migration: the analytics derivation consumer now has
a packet-driven path that produces the same Statistics struct without
re-running per-row SQL aggregations.

What landed:
- Statistics::from_packets(&[ConversationPacket]) -> Statistics
  - Aggregates total_conversations / total_messages / total_characters
    from packet payload + projections (no SQL).
  - Derives per-agent (conversations, messages) buckets from
    payload.identity.agent_slug.
  - Derives per-role buckets from packet.projections.analytics
    (user/assistant/tool/system/other).
  - Earliest/latest started_at from payload.timestamps.started_at.
- AgentStats now derives PartialEq/Eq so equivalence gates can compare
  per-agent buckets structurally.

Equivalence gate (per ibuuh.32 acceptance):
- analytics_statistics_from_packets_matches_sql_for_canonical_corpus
  builds the test corpus once via insert_test_data, runs the legacy
  AnalyticsGenerator::generate_statistics SQL path, then re-builds
  the same corpus as ConversationPackets via from_canonical_replay
  and runs Statistics::from_packets. After normalizing computed_at,
  asserts byte-for-byte JSON equality on every counted field
  (total_*, agents, roles, time_range). A regression on either path's
  role normalization or count semantics fails this test.

11/11 src/pages/analytics.rs lib tests green via
rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/rch_target_cass_p2 cargo test
--lib pages::analytics.

Callers that already hold packets (rebuild pipeline, ledger
publishers) can now derive analytics directly. Migrating the
AnalyticsGenerator main path to consume packets when available is the
next slice; the SQL path stays for callers that only have a DB handle.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
